title: Melted Energizing Indian Citrusy Flaky Cream Garlic Naan
description: A flavorful and energizing twist on the classic Indian flatbread, featuring the brightness of citrus and the richness of double cream.
instructions: Combine yeast and kefir in a large mixing bowl and let sit for 5 minutes until frothy.
Add flour, salt, and fenugreek leaves to the bowl and mix until a shaggy dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5-7 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover it with a damp cloth, and let it rise in a warm place for 1 hour, or until doubled in size.
Preheat a non-stick skillet or griddle over medium heat.
Divide the dough into 6-8 equal pieces and roll each piece into a ball.
Flatten each ball slightly into a disk shape and place in the skillet.
Cook for 1-2 minutes on each side until golden brown, then brush with ghee and serve hot.
Meanwhile, melt 1 tablespoon of butter in a small saucepan over low heat.
Add 2 cloves of minced garlic and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Stir in 1/2 cup of double cream and cook for 2-3 minutes until slightly thickened.
Stir in 2 tablespoons of fresh cilantro and 1 tablespoon of lime juice and cook for another minute.
Brush the melted butter mixture onto the naan breads as they come out of the skillet.
Sprinkle with black pepper to taste.
